question.text: A stockbroker has been found guilty of charging brokerage in excess of that specified in the regulations. If the excess brokerage charged was Rs. 3 crore, what is the maximum penalty that can be imposed under Section 15F of the SEBI Act, 1992?

answer.text: Rs. 15 crore (five times the excess brokerage charged)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 15F of the SEBI Act, the penalty for charging excess brokerage can extend to five times the amount of brokerage charged in excess of the specified brokerage, whichever is higher (compared to one crore). Here, 5 x Rs. 3 crore = Rs. 15 crore, which is higher than Rs. 1 crore. **The Trap:** Rs. 1 crore is the general maximum for other defaults under Section 15F, but excess brokerage has a separate higher penalty formula. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) and NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) also cover penalty provisions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always verify brokerage rates against exchange-prescribed limits to avoid punitive penalties that can be multiples of the excess amount.
